steps.title: Interpret the lower creep rate
steps.content: Creep is deformation under persistent stress, and a lower minimum creep rate means a material deforms more slowly under the tested conditions. The nitrogen-treated fiber therefore suggests better resistance to stress than the two polymer-derived fibers.

steps.title: Avoid overstating the finding
steps.content: Choice A overclaims that only the nitrogen-treated fiber can substantially inhibit creep. Choice C reverses the comparison and adds unsupported chemical-property claims. Choice D says reducing creep resistance would prolong life, which is backwards. Better resistance implies longer stress tolerance in aerospace composites. The answer is B.
